supplementaryLights.title: Shortened braking-distance marker
supplementaryLights.recognition: A steady white supplementary light above the main Ks lamps.
supplementaryLights.meaning: The next announced stopping point is at a braking distance shortened by more than five percent.
playerAction: Treat the displayed warning or speed announcement as having less distance than normal.
Brake promptly enough to meet the announced stop or speed at the required point.

supplementaryLights.title: Distant-signal repeater marker
supplementaryLights.recognition: A steady white supplementary light below the main Ks lamps when the repeated indication is restrictive.
supplementaryLights.meaning: This signal repeats distant information that was already given earlier, usually because the next main signal is difficult to see.
playerAction: Respond to the repeated Ks indication exactly as you would at the original distant indication.
Do not assume a new full braking distance begins at the repeater.
